description:
Root cause of the duplicate flood in 'bl list': every sync poll mis-classifies known issues as AutoCreate and re-creates them. Two coupled defects that must be fixed atomically.

OBSERVED ON DISK (e.g. .balls/worktree/.balls/tasks/bl-9070.json):
  "external": {"github-issues": {"github_issues": {"issue": {...}}}}

Note the double nesting and the hyphen-then-underscore key shift.

DEFECT 1 — double-wrapped projection in sync emit
  File: balls-github-plugin/crates/balls-plugin-github-issues/src/pull_emit.rs, fn created_from (lines ~110-123)

      let mut external = serde_json::Map::new();
      external.insert("github_issues".to_string(), json!({"issue": {...}}));
      SyncCreate { ..., external }

  The plugin already wraps the projection under "github_issues". Then balls-core src/commands/sync_report.rs::apply_created (line 83) wraps it AGAIN under the participant name:

      task.external.insert(plugin_name.to_string(), Value::Object(item.external.clone()));

  Contract per src/plugin/types.rs:6 (PushResponse doc): 'Core stores this verbatim into task.external.{plugin_name}'. The push side already follows the contract correctly (commands/push.rs::issue_blob returns {"issue": {...}} unwrapped). pull_emit should match: just insert "issue" → {...} directly into the SyncCreate.external map (no inner participant wrapper).

DEFECT 2 — accessor key uses underscore, task.external is keyed by hyphenated participant name
  Files: balls-github-plugin/crates/balls-plugin-github-issues/src/config.rs:31 (PROJECTION_PREFIX = "external.github_issues.") and issues_api.rs:88-92 (IssuesTaskExt::issue_blob).

      let key = PROJECTION_PREFIX.strip_prefix("external.").and_then(|s| s.strip_suffix('.')).unwrap();
      self.external.get(key).and_then(|v| v.get("issue"))

  Derived key is 'github_issues' (underscore). But the participant name configured in .balls/config.json is 'github-issues' (hyphen), and that hyphenated form is what apply_created uses as the outer key in task.external. So task.external.get("github_issues") always returns None for any task created via sync.

CASCADING SYMPTOM
  pull::classify (pull.rs:108) calls task.issue_number(), which routes through the broken accessor and returns None for every previously synced task. classify therefore falls through to AutoCreate on every poll, and SyncReport.created re-adds the same GitHub issues every cycle. Current bl list shows 6× 'Fix all remaining map_unwrap_or' (one per sync since the regression), 6× 'Fix map_unwrap_or...on doctor.rs', 2× each release chore v0.2.1 through v0.4.0, etc.

  The push half has the same accessor on the noop check (commands/push.rs:53-61 — task.issue_number() / task.last_synced_status()). Once defect 2 is fixed, push's loop-avoidance starts working too; today a sync'd task pushed locally would create a duplicate GitHub issue.

FIX SHAPE
  Both defects must be repaired together — fixing either alone leaves the projection unreadable.
  1. pull_emit::created_from: insert {"issue": <value>} directly into SyncCreate.external (no "github_issues" wrapper).
  2. Align the participant name and PROJECTION_PREFIX. Options:
     a. Rename the .balls config plugin key to 'github_issues' (underscore), keep PROJECTION_PREFIX = 'external.github_issues.'. Simplest for the plugin but is a user-visible config rename.
     b. Change PROJECTION_PREFIX to 'external.github-issues.' (hyphen). Plugin-internal, no operator action needed.
     Recommend (b): the configured participant name is the authoritative key in task.external (per types.rs PushResponse doc), so accessors should follow it.
  3. Add a regression test: a round-trip where a sync-create followed by classify on a re-poll yields KnownUpdate (or Skip via loop-avoidance), never AutoCreate.

Companion ball to clean up the existing 40+ duplicate tasks once the fix lands.
title: github-issues sync: SyncCreate.external is double-wrapped and accessor key mismatches participant name [bl-a2ea]
